page: Amazon Web Services c5.18xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8223CL processor with x86_64 architecture, offering 36 physical cores and 72 vCPUs with dedicated CPU allocation. The processor operates at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz and includes 24 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 144.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU scoring 66,687.24 and PassMark CPU scoring 49,988.17, both placing in the top 25 percent of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is also highly efficient, reaching 208.99 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 92.77 GB/sec for large-block write operations. Database operations and key-value store performance are notable, with Redis SET operations achieving 60.97 million operations per second. However, single-core metrics and text generation speeds for large language models fall into average or weak categories, with text generation for a medium 7B model limited to 6.95 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c5.18xlarge provides high compute density and strong multi-threaded performance relative to its memory footprint. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it highly suitable for compute-bound workloads rather than memory-intensive applications. Tradeoffs include the lack of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU hardware, which limits its utility for deep learning training or high-speed local caching. It is best suited for workloads such as batch processing, distributed analytics, high-performance web serving, and database operations that leverage its strong multi-core capabilities.
description: The Amazon Web Services c5.18xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8223CL processor with 72 vCPUs and 36 physical cores running at 3.4 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 144.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but offers a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results show strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth, alongside high-throughput Redis SET operations. However, single-core performance and LLM text generation speeds are moderate to weak. Given its high compute density and lack of local storage, the c5.18xlarge is highly cost-efficient for compute-bound, multi-threaded workloads such as batch processing, distributed analytics, and database operations rather than memory-heavy or GPU-accelerated tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5.18xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 72 vCPUs, 144 GB DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 3.4 GHz processor, delivering strong multi-core performance for compute-bound workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5.18xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 72 vCPUs, 144 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 3.4 GHz Intel Xeon CPU on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized virtual server featuring seventy-two dedicated vCPUs and strong multi-core performance for demanding parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 72 vCPUs and 36 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8223CL processor at 3.4 GHz
144.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing a compute-optimized 2.0 GB per core ratio
Strong multi-core CPU performance and high-throughput memory bandwidth in benchmarks
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU acceleration
Optimized for batch processing, distributed analytics, and database operations
